backend/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Spring Boot parent version to 3.3.13 to align with Java 21 and modern Spring ecosystem.
backend/pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 2.x does not support Java 21 as a baseline; upgrading is required to run on JDK 21.

backend/pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property to 21 to target the new JDK.
backend/pom.xml.why_exists: The project must declare Java 21 to compile and run on the target JDK.

backend/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade maven-surefire-plugin to a version compatible with the new build environment.
Upgrade jacoco-maven-plugin to a version compatible with the new build environment.
Remove explicit version from groovy dependency to let Spring Boot manage it.
Exclude junit from groovy-all to avoid dependency conflicts with Spring Boot's managed JUnit version.
Remove explicit version from h2 dependency to let Spring Boot manage it.
Replace source/target compiler configuration with release flag for modern Java compilation.
backend/pom.xml.why_exists: The source/target configuration is deprecated in newer JDKs; release is the standard way to specify target compatibility.

backend/src/main/java/pt/ulisboa/tecnico.general_idea: Migrate JPA annotations from the legacy javax.persistence package to the Jakarta EE 9+ jakarta.persistence package.
backend/src/main/java/pt/ulisboa/tecnico.why_exists: The javax.persistence package is removed in Jakarta EE 9+ and Spring Boot 3.x, causing a compile error 'package javax.persistence does not exist' on Java 21 with the upgraded framework.
